A dark branch of evolution, tainted by v: A dark branch of evolution, tainted by void-tech. Corruption twists its frame, reactor howls like a collapsing star. It moves with unnatural spasms—unstable, terrifying, yet overwhelmingly powerful.
A glowing fortress of shields and radian: A glowing fortress of shields and radiant plating. Built to withstand orbital bombardments, this mech inspires entire armies with its unyielding defense and brilliant aura.
A patched-up wreck, limping forward with: A patched-up wreck, limping forward with mismatched armor and screeching servos. Every step is agony, but its core refuses to shut down. It is stubborn survival embodied in steel.

web.aas.allowed_users_hint: Only trigger if message is from these users (comma-separated)
web.aas.allowed_users_tooltip: Discord User/Bot ID (not username). Right-click user → Copy ID. Enable Developer Mode in Discord settings if needed.
web.aas.audit_channel_hint: Channel where all AAS events will be logged.
web.aas.cooldown_tooltip: Prevents spam from rapid updates. After triggering, the rule won't fire again for this duration.
web.aas.delay_tooltip: Wait time before action executes. Useful when server needs time to save data before restart.
web.aas.feedback_channels_hint: Select one or more channels for action feedback.
web.aas.global_cooldown_hint: Minimum time between ANY auto-action executions. Prevents spam.
web.aas.ignore_keywords_tooltip: Prevents false triggers. E.g. 'driver issue', 'hotfix failed', 'rollback' won't trigger even if other keywords match.
web.aas.monitored_channels_tooltip: We recommend using a dedicated channel for Auto-Actions, especially when not using Allowed User IDs. This prevents unintended triggers from other messages.
web.aas.only_if_running: Only execute if container is RUNNING
web.aas.only_if_running_tooltip: Safety feature: Prevents accidentally starting containers that were intentionally stopped for maintenance.
web.aas.only_webhooks: Only trigger on Webhook messages (e.g. Discohook)
web.aas.protected_containers_hint: Comma-separated list of containers that AAS cannot touch (case-insensitive).
web.aas.required_keywords_tooltip: Use your product/game name here (e.g. 'Icarus'). This prevents false triggers from other sources. The message title/embed is also searched.
web.aas.target_containers_hint: Select one or more containers to apply the action to.
web.aas.title: Auto-Action System (AAS)
web.aas.trigger_keywords_hint: At least one must match (OR logic)
web.action_log.description: This log tracks user-initiated actions like Start, Stop, Restart, or clearing this log itself. It persists across container restarts (if stored in a volume) and is not subject to Docker's automatic log rotation. It will only be cleared when you click the "Clear Log" button below. The main container log (viewable with docker logs dockerdiscordcontrol or in the section above) is managed by Docker and may be rotated or cleared based on Docker settings.
web.action_log.heading: User Action Log
web.advanced.bg_refresh_limit_tooltip: Maximum number of containers to refresh per background cycle. Prevents overwhelming the system when you have many containers.
web.advanced.cache_ttl_tooltip: How long cached container data stays valid before being considered stale. Automatically calculated as Status Update Interval × 2.5 for optimal performance.
web.advanced.container_list_timeout_tool: Docker API: Timeout for fetching the complete container list from Docker. If Docker daemon is slow, this prevents hanging operations.
web.advanced.description: Advanced settings for fine-tuning performance. Changes require container restart to take effect.
web.advanced.difficulty_dynamic: Dynamic evolution costs based on community size (min 1.0x)
web.advanced.difficulty_saved_immediatel: Changes saved immediately • Achieved levels stay locked forever
web.advanced.disable_donation_key_toolti: Enter a valid key to completely remove all donation buttons, mech animations, and Power bars from both Web UI and Discord. Purchase key at Buy Me a Coffee.
web.advanced.docker_max_cache_age_hint: Force refresh after this time regardless of TTL
web.advanced.docker_max_cache_age_toolti: Maximum age before cached Docker data is forcibly refreshed, regardless of normal cache TTL. Safety mechanism for critical operations.
web.advanced.docker_query_cooldown_hint: Prevents API spam from simultaneous commands
web.advanced.docker_query_cooldown_toolt: Minimum time between Docker API calls for the same container. Prevents API spam when multiple Discord users trigger commands simultaneously.
web.advanced.donation_save_to_apply: Save the configuration to apply changes. All donation elements will be hidden.
web.advanced.donation_system_disabled_de: All donation elements are hidden in Web UI and Discord. Remove the key below to re-enable donations.
web.advanced.enable_bg_refresh_hint: Auto-refresh container list (disable for resource savings)
web.advanced.enable_bg_refresh_tooltip: Web UI: Automatically refresh container list in background. Disable if you have many containers or limited resources.
web.advanced.enable_live_logs_tooltip: Discord: Allow users to view live container logs in Discord. Disable to reduce Discord API usage and prevent log spam.
web.advanced.evolution_cost_multiplier_t: Adjusts the difficulty of mech evolution costs with 20 granular steps. Easy (0.5x) makes evolution half price, Hard (2.0x) makes it double price. Achieved levels stay locked forever.
web.advanced.fast_stats_timeout_hint: Quick stats timeout for responsive commands
web.advanced.fast_stats_timeout_tooltip: Docker API: Timeout for quick container stats (CPU, memory). Used for responsive Discord commands. If containers don't respond within this time, they're marked as slow.
web.advanced.key_invalid_purchase: Invalid license key. Purchase a valid license at Buy Me a Coffee.
web.advanced.key_status_default: Enter key to disable all donation features
web.advanced.key_status_invalid: Invalid license key - purchase at Buy Me a Coffee
web.advanced.key_status_valid: Valid license key - donations will be disabled
web.advanced.key_validated: License key validated! Donations will be completely disabled.
web.advanced.logs_auto_start_hint: Start logs immediately vs. manual control
web.advanced.logs_auto_start_tooltip: Discord: Automatically start live log updates when someone clicks the logs button. If disabled, users need to manually start/stop.
web.advanced.logs_max_refreshes_tooltip: Discord: Maximum number of times to refresh live logs before auto-stopping. Prevents infinite Discord API calls.
web.advanced.logs_message_timeout_toolti: Discord: How long to keep live log messages active before auto-deletion. Keeps Discord channels clean.
web.advanced.logs_refresh_interval_toolt: Discord: How often to update live log messages with new container log lines. Lower values = more real-time logs, higher Discord API usage.
web.advanced.logs_tail_lines_hint: Log lines per message (watch Discord limits)
web.advanced.logs_tail_lines_tooltip: Discord: Number of recent log lines to show per container. Higher values show more history but may hit Discord message length limits.
web.advanced.max_concurrent_tasks_toolti: Maximum number of scheduled tasks (container actions) that can run simultaneously. Prevents system overload during mass operations.
web.advanced.max_containers_display_hint: Container list display limit for performance
web.advanced.max_containers_display_tool: Maximum number of containers to show in the Web UI. Higher numbers may slow down page loading. Does not affect Discord Bot functionality.
web.advanced.purchase_key_hint: Purchase a key to completely remove all donation elements
web.advanced.refresh_interval_tooltip: Web UI: How often to refresh the container list in the Web UI dashboard. Separate from Discord Bot refresh. Higher values reduce server load.
web.advanced.refresh_timeout_hint: Skip slow containers after this timeout
web.advanced.refresh_timeout_tooltip: Timeout for each container refresh operation. If a container takes longer than this to respond, it's skipped to prevent blocking.
web.advanced.scheduler_check_interval_to: How often the scheduler checks for pending tasks (start/stop/restart commands). Lower values = more responsive scheduled actions, higher CPU usage.
web.advanced.slow_stats_timeout_tooltip: Docker API: Extended timeout for containers that were previously slow. Gives more time for detailed stats without blocking fast containers.
web.advanced.static_difficulty_override_: Enable to override dynamic community-based evolution costs with static custom difficulty. When OFF, costs adjust based on Discord community size (minimum 1.0x). When ON, use your custom slider settings. Achieved levels always stay locked.
web.advanced.status_update_interval_hint: Container status refresh for commands. Recommended: 30-60s
web.advanced.status_update_interval_tool: Discord Bot: How often to fetch fresh container status for Discord commands (/serverstatus, /control). Lower = more responsive bot, higher CPU usage.
web.advanced.task_batch_size_tooltip: Number of tasks to process together in one scheduler cycle. Higher values = more efficient processing, but may cause delays for some tasks.
web.advanced.title: Advanced Settings
web.auth.description: Configure the password for accessing this web interface. The username is fixed to admin. If you forget the password, you can manually edit the config.json file and either remove the web_ui_password_hash line (which will reset it to the default 'admin' on next startup) or replace the hash value.
web.auth.heading: Web UI Authentication
web.auth.new_password_label: New Password (leave blank to keep current)
web.botsetup.additional_help: For additional help and resources, visit
web.botsetup.after_guide_note: After completing this guide, you'll need to save the Bot Token and Server (Guild) ID in your DDC configuration.
web.botsetup.before_begin_desc: This guide will help you set up the Discord Bot portion of DockerDiscordControl. You'll need:
web.botsetup.heading: DockerDiscordControl Bot Setup Guide
web.botsetup.inviting_bot_title: Inviting Your Bot to Your Server
web.botsetup.req_admin_perms: Administrator permissions on the Discord server where you want to use the bot
web.botsetup.slash_commands_note_desc: After adding your bot to the server, slash commands may not be immediately available. Discord can take up to one hour to fully register all commands across their network. If commands don't appear right away, please be patient.
web.botsetup.subheading: Complete step-by-step instructions to create and configure your Discord Bot
web.channel.guild_id_tooltip: The ID of your Discord server. Enable Developer Mode in Discord (Settings -> Advanced), then right-click on your server in the server list -> 'Copy ID'. This helps the bot register commands faster.
web.config.aas_description: Automatically trigger actions based on Discord messages (e.g., game updates).
web.config.donation_appeal: If you find DDC helpful, consider supporting its development:
web.config.donation_enter_details: Please enter your donation details to power the mech:
web.config.power_mech_cta: Power the Mech with your donations to bring it to life!
web.config.restart_warning: After saving, the Docker container must be restarted manually (e.g., via Unraid UI or docker restart ddc) for the bot to apply all changes!
web.config.save_note: Note: The configuration is split into multiple files. After saving, the container should be restarted to apply changes such as token, channel IDs, or server selection!
web.config.scheduled_tasks_description: Manage scheduled actions for your containers.
web.ct.source_channel_hint: The channel to monitor for new messages.
web.ct.target_channel_hint: The channel where translations will be posted.
web.ct.translate_embeds: Translate Embed Content (titles, descriptions from bots/webhooks)
web.diagnostics.description: Run diagnostics to troubleshoot Web UI connection issues, port mappings, and system configuration.
web.diagnostics.issue_firewall_hint: Ensure port 8374 is open on host system
web.diagnostics.issue_unraid_hint: Edit DDC container → Set WebUI port correctly
web.diagnostics.title: System Diagnostics
web.discord.bot_token_tooltip: Find your bot token in the Discord Developer Portal under your Bot Application -> 'Bot' -> 'Token'. Click 'Reset Token' (if necessary) and then 'Copy'. Treat this token like a password!
web.donations.confirm_permanent: This is permanent and cannot be undone!
web.donations.delete_detail_4: Member counts freeze at correct moments
web.donations.event_sourcing_desc: Deleting a donation adds a compensation event and recalculates all levels and costs correctly.
web.donations.no_donations: No donations found in the history.
web.donations.title: Donation Management
web.heartbeat.how_it_works_description: DDC periodically pings an external monitoring URL. If the ping stops, the monitoring service sends you an alert.
web.heartbeat.interval_help: Recommended: 5 minutes. Set the monitoring service timeout to ~15 minutes.
web.heartbeat.interval_tooltip: How often DDC sends a ping to the monitoring URL. Set the monitoring service timeout to at least 2-3x this value.
web.heartbeat.label: Status Watchdog
web.heartbeat.label_tooltip: Monitors if the DDC bot is still running by periodically pinging an external monitoring service.
web.heartbeat.monitoring_url_help: Must start with https://. Only a simple GET request is sent - no tokens or data.
web.heartbeat.monitoring_url_tooltip: The URL that DDC will ping periodically. Must be HTTPS. Examples: https://hc-ping.com/your-uuid (Healthchecks.io) or https://your-uptime-kuma.com/api/push/xxx (Uptime Kuma). No data is sent - just a simple HTTP GET request.
web.heartbeat.security_description: This is safer than traditional monitoring because no bot tokens are shared. DDC only makes outbound HTTPS requests (like Discord API calls) - no incoming connections, no data exposure.
web.heartbeat.step1: Create a free account at Healthchecks.io or host Uptime Kuma
web.heartbeat.step2: Create a new "check" and copy the ping URL
web.heartbeat.step3: Paste the URL below and set your desired interval
web.heartbeat.step4: Configure alerts in the monitoring service (Email, Discord Webhook, etc.)
web.lang.bot_language_help: Language used by the bot in Discord.
web.lang.channel_translation_desc: Automatically translate messages between channels using DeepL, Google, or Microsoft.
web.lang.description: Configure language, timezone, and channel translation settings.
web.lang.no_pairs_help: Click "Add Pair" to create your first channel translation.
web.lang.title: Language and Timezone
web.lang.ui_language_help: Language used for the web interface.
web.logs.debug_level_help: Toggle between Debug and Info log levels. Debug shows detailed system information, Info shows standard messages.
web.logs.description: View and manage different log types from your DockerDiscordControl system.
web.logs.heading: System Logs
web.permissions.admin_id_help: To get a Discord User ID: Enable Developer Mode in Discord, right-click on user, and select "Copy User ID"
web.permissions.admin_modal_desc: Admin users have full container control in Status Channels, including Start/Stop/Restart buttons, Task system, Info system, and Admin Control Panel (🛠️ button).
web.permissions.admin_modal_note: This only affects Status Channels. In Control Channels, ALL users have full access regardless of admin status.
web.permissions.admin_users_desc: Full access to Start, Stop, Restart buttons, Task system, Info system, and Admin Control Panel
web.permissions.all_users_desc: Can use /serverstatus command to view container status
web.permissions.col_channel_id_tooltip: The Discord channel ID. Enable Developer Mode in Discord, then right-click the channel and select Copy ID
web.permissions.col_channel_name_tooltip: A display name to help you remember which channel this is
web.permissions.col_initial_control_tool: Post initial control messages on bot start?
web.permissions.col_initial_tooltip: Post initial status messages on bot start?
web.permissions.col_minutes_recreate_too: Recreate messages after X minutes of inactivity.
web.permissions.col_minutes_refresh_tool: Update status messages every X minutes (if refresh is enabled).
web.permissions.control_channels_desc: Control channels provide full container management capabilities to ALL users in the channel.
web.permissions.security_warning_desc: ALL users in control channels have unrestricted access to ALL container management functions including Start, Stop, Restart, Task system, and Info editing. Only add trusted channels here!
web.permissions.status_channels_desc: Status channels display container status messages with restricted control access.
web.secure_token.auto_clear_desc: This modal will automatically clear the token and close after 5 minutes for security.
web.secure_token.compose_step1: Create or edit .env file in your project directory
web.secure_token.compose_step4: Verify: Check logs for "Using environment variable" message
web.secure_token.docker_step1: Stop current container: docker stop ddc
web.secure_token.docker_step4: Or update your existing run command with -e DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=your_token
web.secure_token.portainer_step1: Go to Containers → ddc container → Edit
web.secure_token.portainer_step4: Set value to the token copied above
web.secure_token.security_notice_bold: Your Discord bot token will be displayed below.
web.secure_token.security_notice_desc: Keep this token secure and never share it publicly. Anyone with this token can control your Discord bot.
web.secure_token.title: Secure Token Migration
web.secure_token.token_hint: Click the eye icon to show/hide the token. Use the Copy button to securely copy it to your clipboard.
web.secure_token.unraid_step1: Go to Docker tab → ddc container → Edit
web.secure_token.unraid_step4: Set value to the token copied above
web.server.access_password_help: Users will need this password to see the protected information
web.server.access_password_placeholder: Password required to view protected content
web.server.chars_help: characters - Use line breaks for multiple entries
web.server.col_active_tooltip: Should the bot manage this container?
web.server.col_display_name_tooltip: This name will be shown in the Discord Bot.
web.server.custom_ip_help: Leave empty to use auto-detected public IP
web.server.custom_port_help: Optional: Port number for the server
web.server.custom_text_placeholder:
Password: mypass123
Mods: ModName1, ModName2
Max Players: 8
web.server.description: Select containers (check 'Active') and define the display name and allowed bot actions.
web.server.enable_protected_help: Content that requires a password to view in Discord
web.server.protected_chars_help: characters - This content will be password protected
web.server.protected_content_placeholder: Admin passwords, secret IPs, sensitive information...
web.server.title: Container Selection & Bot Permissions
web.setup.err_fill_both: Please fill in both password fields
web.setup.err_password_too_short: Password must be at least 6 characters long
web.setup.password_requirements: Password must be at least 6 characters long
web.setup.security_note: Your password will be securely hashed using PBKDF2-SHA256
web.setup.welcome_subtitle: Set up your admin password to get started
web.spam.description: Configure rate limiting to prevent spam and abuse of bot commands.
web.spam.title: Spam Protection Settings
web.tasks.action_help: Action to apply to the container.
web.tasks.container_help: Name of the Docker container to control.
web.tasks.cron_format_help: Format: minute hour day month weekday
web.tasks.err_monthly_day: For monthly tasks, please enter a valid day of the month (1-31)
web.tasks.err_once_date_required: For one-time tasks, you must specify day, month, and year
web.tasks.err_once_future_date: One-time tasks must be scheduled for a future date/time
web.tasks.form_description: Define tasks to be executed on a schedule (e.g., restart a container daily).
web.tasks.inactive_note: Inactive tasks will not be executed
web.tasks.list_description: List of tasks currently scheduled for execution.
web.token_security.benefit_1: Higher security than encrypted config files
web.token_security.benefit_2: Works with Unraid, Docker Compose, Kubernetes
web.token_security.benefit_3: Token never stored in files or backups
web.token_security.confirm_encrypt: Encrypt the bot token using your admin password? This will make the token more secure.
web.token_security.error_checking_status: Could not check token security status.
web.token_security.status_excellent_desc: Using environment variable DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN. This is the most secure method.
web.token_security.status_good_desc: Bot token is encrypted in config file using your admin password.
web.token_security.status_no_token_desc: Please configure your Discord bot token to enable DDC.
web.token_security.status_risk_desc: Bot token is stored in plaintext. Consider encrypting it for better security.
web.token_security.title: Bot Token Security Status
web.token_security.upgrade_desc: Migrate to environment variable for enhanced security. This is the industry standard for Docker deployments and provides better protection than encrypted config files.

• Automatic timezone detection from Web:
• Automatic timezone detection from Web UI config
• No more hardcoded timezones
• Better token decryption
• Dynamically configurable cooldowns for:
• Dynamically configurable cooldowns for all commands
• Web UI Modal under 'Web UI Authentication'
• Individual settings per command and button
• Protection against rate-limiting and abuse
• New /info command for detailed contain:
• New /info command for detailed container information
• Port field in info editor modal
• Live WAN IP detection or custom address
• Consistent display in all info views
